title: fails to substitute ARG in Dockerfile
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Buildah fails to substitute variables in some cases

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `git clone https://github.com/XVilka/alpine-dev-env`

2.  `./build`

3. `./run`

4. `cat /etc/pkg/repositories`

**Describe the results you received:**

The last `cat` commands shows this content:
```
/home/${username}/packages/main
...
```
Which means clearly it didn't subsittute argument in this sed invocation:
```Dockerfile
ARG username=akochkov

RUN sed -i.bkp -e \
	'1s;^;/home/${username}/packages/testing\n;' \
	/etc/apk/repositories && \
	sed -i.bkp -e \
	'1s;^;/home/${username}/packages/community\n;' \
	/etc/apk/repositories && \
	sed -i.bkp -e \
	'1s;^;/home/${username}/packages/main\n;' \
	/etc/apk/repositories
```

This sed invocation didn't substitute the variables either:
```Dockerfile
RUN sed -i.bkp -e \
	's/#PACKAGER="Your Name <your@email.address>"/PACKAGER="${realname} <${email}>"/g' \
	/etc/abuild.conf
RUN sed -i.bkp -e \
	's/#MAINTAINER="$PACKAGER"/MAINTAINER="$PACKAGER"/g' \
	/etc/abuild.conf
```

Note, if I change the sed commands to use double quote, it works fine, e.g.:
```Dockerfile
ARG username=akochkov

RUN sed -i.bkp -e \
	"1s;^;/home/${username}/packages/testing\n;" \
	/etc/apk/repositories && \
	sed -i.bkp -e \
	"1s;^;/home/${username}/packages/community\n;" \
	/etc/apk/repositories && \
	sed -i.bkp -e \
	"1s;^;/home/${username}/packages/main\n;" \
	/etc/apk/repositories
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
/home/akochkov/packages/main
...
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version: 1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version: 1
Go Version: go1.13.1
OS/Arch: linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
Note, that in case of using `ARG` in `MAINTAINER` it also fails to substitute:
```Dockerfile
ARG realname="Some Name"
ARG email="some.name@gmail.com"
FROM alpine:edge
MAINTAINER ${realname} <${email}>
```
and then if you inspect the resulting image with `podman image inspect <ID> | grep "author"` it will show that substitution didn't happen:
```
"author": "${realname} \u003c${email}\u003e",
...
```

@XVilka I think this is an issue with the quoting around the sed commands in the script.  At least that's the theory.

First off, I was not able to run your sample locally as a number of files that it tried to copy such as anton*.rsa, anton*.rsa.pub and several others were not available.  So I cut down your Dockerfile to :  
```
# cat ~/Dockerfile.tom
FROM alpine:edge
MAINTAINER Anton Kochkov <anton.kochkov@gmail.com>

# Some variables
ARG USERNAME=akochkov
ARG REALNAME="Anton Kochkov"
ARG EMAIL="anton.kochkov@gmail.com"
ARG KEYNAME="anton.kochkov@gmail.com-5db6cdc1"

# Enable "testing" repository
RUN echo "http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/edge/testing" >> \
	/etc/apk/repositories

RUN apk add --no-cache alpine-sdk bash bash-doc bash-completion \
	zsh zsh-vcs coreutils man python3 python3-dev tmux neovim tig mc hub
RUN addgroup -g 1000 -S user && adduser -u 1000 -D -S ${USERNAME} -G user -G abuild
RUN echo "${USERNAME} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" >> /etc/sudoers

# Write abuild configuration
RUN sed -i.bkp -e \
	"s/#PACKAGER=\"Your Name <your@email.address>\"/PACKAGER=\"${REALNAME} <${EMAIL}>\"/g" \
	/etc/abuild.conf
RUN sed -i.bkp -e \
	's/#MAINTAINER="$PACKAGER"/MAINTAINER="$PACKAGER"/g' \
	/etc/abuild.conf
```
and then run it:
```
# buildah bud --layers -t tom -f ~/Dockerfile.tom .
STEP 1: FROM alpine:edge
STEP 2: MAINTAINER Anton Kochkov <anton.kochkov@gmail.com>
--> Using cache b843f5639fc34f7ea7d156ab64bca97d65ab77b24eb6172255f0e9ee2421040e

--> Using cache 5ea422ba12a95b820f127ea9a479748fb652e7a83697bf06b85521e91ebfcee6

--> Using cache 6999877413a69b63cbe2bde5fda11901fc6de4b0f7aa49180da263f7b30d8f6b

--> Using cache a82a53d3fd6679a7fae0fdcd79956ebb4f75c2be707a54db87dc868df7a925d1

--> Using cache 6a7066132cad18f2cbc5993aa36334775adf5ea4bfb5f36cba9967187eba2c73
STEP 8: RUN apk add --no-cache alpine-sdk bash bash-doc bash-completion 	zsh zsh-vcs coreutils man python3 python3-dev tmux neovim tig mc hub
--> Using cache 833bf682181f6f8598dae4e7f69561cb5edb6d9aa61a9e6c9b986bb115f315ec
STEP 9: RUN addgroup -g 1000 -S user && adduser -u 1000 -D -S ${USERNAME} -G user -G abuild
--> Using cache 5bb2f02b6630de7fae681fc110a7b8d8dee51f26b592706ba56d40ad309dd7be
STEP 10: RUN echo "${USERNAME} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" >> /etc/sudoers
--> Using cache 294f5de7fe426303343539a6601a038badb4ece32f4b640333e62cc7f665449f
STEP 11: RUN sed -i.bkp -e 	"s/#PACKAGER=\"Your Name <your@email.address>\"/PACKAGER=\"${REALNAME} <${EMAIL}>\"/g" 	/etc/abuild.conf
066d66c41f008057e08700374508f3db24773f00a472ac9045e95892adb0ef9d
STEP 12: RUN sed -i.bkp -e 	's/#MAINTAINER="$PACKAGER"/MAINTAINER="$PACKAGER"/g' 	/etc/abuild.conf
STEP 13: COMMIT tom
5b498c077e76100a0f527d977372a9f412d6e8f9ed97fe3910162cff31467145
```
And then run the container and check the file, I see the sed changes as intended with the variable expansion:
```
# buildah run tom-working-container /bin/bash
bash-5.0# cat /etc/abuild.conf
export CFLAGS="-Os -fomit-frame-pointer"
{extra lines removed for brevity}
# PACKAGER and MAINTAINER are used by newapkbuild when creating new aports for
# the APKBUILD's "Contributor:" and "Maintainer:" comments, respectively.
PACKAGER="Anton Kochkov <anton.kochkov@gmail.com>"
MAINTAINER="$PACKAGER"
{extra lines removed for brevity}
```

The difference being the way the sed line was commented in the Dockerfile.  Originally it was:

```
RUN sed -i.bkp -e \
	's/#PACKAGER="Your Name <your@email.address>"/PACKAGER="${REALNAME} <${EMAIL}>"/g' \
	/etc/abuild.conf
```

and I saw the same issue that you were, and it worked when I changed that line to:
```
RUN sed -i.bkp -e \
	"s/#PACKAGER=\"Your Name <your@email.address>\"/PACKAGER=\"${REALNAME} <${EMAIL}>\"/g" \
	/etc/abuild.conf
```

Can you try making similar changes to the script and see if it helps?
@TomSweeneyRedHat yes, it works if I change the quotation. But it worked well in the original docker with the single quotation marks. So I think it should be fixed still.
Thanks for the info @XVilka , I'll poke around with Docker then to see where that stands.  I'm not sure how much I can change the handling of quotes in sed commands like that, but will look.
whoops, didn't mean to hit the close button.

That looks like we are not handling shell expansion correctly.

Is our code not changing ${REALNAME} in this case?

```
RUN sed -i.bkp -e \
	's/#PACKAGER="Your Name <your@email.address>"/PACKAGER="${REALNAME} <${EMAIL}>"/g' \
	/etc/abuild.conf
```

The other interesting thing to note is that in Docker, ARGs that appear before the FROM statement don't exist after the FROM statement. I noticed that `podman build  .` has the ARGs exist throughout the Dockerfile.

Reference: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/

#### Understand how ARG and FROM interact

FROM instructions support variables that are declared by any ARG instructions that occur before the first FROM.

```dockerfile
ARG  CODE_VERSION=latest
FROM base:${CODE_VERSION}
CMD  /code/run-app
FROM extras:${CODE_VERSION}
CMD  /code/run-extras
```

An ARG declared before a FROM is outside of a build stage, so it can’t be used in any instruction after a FROM. To use the default value of an ARG declared before the first FROM use an ARG instruction without a value inside of a build stage:

```dockerfile
ARG VERSION=latest
FROM busybox:$VERSION
ARG VERSION
RUN echo $VERSION > image_version
```
@hreysenbach I think your concern in https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1957#issuecomment-564093447 should be resolved as of https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2220.
